vulnerability.title: Verify Permissions on Backup shadow File
vulnerability.description: To properly set the permissions of /etc/shadow-, run the command: $ sudo chmod 0000 /etc/shadow-

triage_decision.reason: The finding involves setting restrictive permissions on /etc/shadow-, a backup file for password hashes. This is a standard STIG hardening practice for file permissions, with minimal risk of lockout or service breakage since it's not the active shadow file and follows explicit policy for safe remediation of file permissions.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; filesystems affected; file_permissions; authentication
